name: Unsafe Consumption of APIs
what_it_means: Trusting upstream/external APIs without validation, TLS checks, or safe failure allows data poisoning, spoofing, or logic abuse.
animated_example: Eating mystery food without checking the label — could be spoiled or mislabeled.
quick_checks: Validate upstream responses against strict schemas/types
Fail securely (no fail-open); sanitize before use
Cache poisoning protections; verify source authenticity
how_to_test: Manipulated nested objects; missing required fields
expected_outcomes: Graceful failure with safe defaults; no state corruption
No leakage of upstream internals; events logged
minimal_example: Upstream /profiles returns unexpected fields → local service rejects and logs; operation aborted
mitigation: Validate all upstream API responses against a strict JSON schema before any processing — reject responses with unexpected fields, incorrect types, or sizes exceeding defined thresholds

Enforce TLS with full certificate chain validation for all external integrations; never disable certificate checks in production code, and consider certificate pinning for high-trust third-party services
Implement timeouts, retries with exponential backoff, and circuit breakers for every external API dependency — design all integrations to fail closed (deny by default on error, not fail open)

Sanitize and re-encode all data received from upstream services before passing it to downstream systems, databases, or rendering layers to prevent injection attacks, cache poisoning, and data integrity violations
